excerpt:
Rocky Mountain Area(PL 4)   
Fire Activity and Teams Assigned Totals 
New fires: 38 
New large incidents: 0 
Uncontained large fires: 8 
CIMTs Committed: 5 
 
Aspen Acres, Custer County. CIMT (NR Team 6). Twenty miles southwest of Pueblo, CO. Timber. Moderate 
fire behavior with short crown runs, flanking and single tree torching. Numerous residences and 
communication infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Fishhook, Medicine Bow-Routt NF, USFS. CIMT (SW Team 1). Eight miles southeast of Steamboat Springs, 
CO. Grass, brush and timber. Minimal fire behavior with creeping, group torching and smoldering. Energy 
infrastructure threatened. Area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Gold Mountain, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (RM Team 2). Two miles north 
of Ouray, CO. Grass, brush and timber. Moderate fire behavior with flanking, backing and torching. Numerous 
structures and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Elk, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (NW Team 8). Seven miles northwest of 
Lake City, CO. Timber, heavy slash and grass. Minimal fire behavior with flanking, backing and isolated 
torching. Residences threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. Precipitation occurred 
over the fire area yesterday. 
 
Willow, Pike and San Isabel NF, USFS. CIMT (CA Team 8). Six miles west of Leadville, CO. Brush, closed 
timber litter and timber. Moderate fire behavior with creeping, isolated torching and smoldering. Residences 
and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. Precipitation occurred 
over the fire area yesterday. 
 
Ferris, San Juan NF, USFS. Seven miles east of Cahone, CO. Timber and brush. Minimal fire behavior with 
smoldering. Area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
P L Gulch, White River Field Office, BLM. Nineteen miles southwest of Meeker, CO. Brush, grass and timber. 
Minimal fire behavior with backing, creeping and smoldering. Structures and energy infrastructure threatened. 
 
Big Gulch, Moffat County. Eight miles northwest of Craig, CO. Grass and brush. Moderate fire behavior with 
smoldering and creeping. Reduction in acreage due to more accurate mapping.
